_comment: Art direction for Kithbound — the one place to re-skin the whole game. 'palette' is the shared set of colors the world AND the characters draw from; edit these to change the entire look. 'light.dir' is a single direction everything is lit from (up-left by default), which is what makes the scene feel cohesive — highlights land on that side, shadows fall opposite. 'characters'/'entities' choose procedural drawing or your own art: set render to 'sprite' and point 'sprite' at a res:// image to drop in art you made yourself (a missing file silently falls back to procedural). Trees take it further: instead of one 'sprite' they can name a 'trunk' and a 'canopy' image, and only the canopy sways in the wind while the trunk stays planted (see tools/gen_trees.py). Colors are [r,g,b] or [r,g,b,a] in 0..1. Presentation-only: none of this touches game logic.

entities._water_comment: Water is a SURFACE, not a silhouette: one seamless tile (tools/gen_water.py) that WorldArt tiles across each pond/river/pool and scrolls a texel at a time. 'tile' names the image; 'world_tile' is how many world units one tile spans (bigger = chunkier pixels); a missing tile falls back to the old flat fill. See tools/pixelart/water.py.
entities._portal_comment: A neutral (grayscale) energy oval (tools/gen_portal.py); WorldArt tints it per-portal with the portal's own color and keeps the breathing pulse + orbiting sparks procedural. A missing file falls back to the procedural shimmering ovals.

_daycycle_comment: A slow ambient drift from day to dusk and back, driving the warm wash (DayTint) and the vignette. enabled=false freezes it at the first stop (today's static look). period_sec is one full day->dusk->day round trip when loop=true. Set enabled=false to turn it off.
